cycle_semantics: emitted Cairo opcode cycles; expected 7 * fib_n + 16

measurement.process_model: fresh gpu_bench process per proof; every subprocess uses --reps 1 --reuse-input
measurement.execution_policy: strictly sequential with alternating backend and size order
measurement.proof_acceptance: each process emits one record only after verify_cairo returns and must exit zero
measurement.end_to_end_scope: subprocess launch through verified process exit

excludes: proof size calculation and JSON reporting

limitations: Native Cairo cycle MHz is comparable only between backends proving this exact Cairo program and protocol.
Cross-VM Fib comparisons must use fib iterations per second because VM cycle definitions differ.
gpu_bench rounds its internal phase timings to milliseconds.
No result is synthesized; every reported lane is an executed gpu_bench backend.
The Metal lane is accepted only as metal/legacy, matching gpu_bench's implemented dispatch table.

raw_samples.verification_evidence: gpu_bench emitted its sole record after verify_cairo returned
